product: NetApp ONTAP REST API — volume snapshot inventory and retention

auth.note: sent on every request; session object never exposes a plaintext password property

list_snapshots.pagination_rule: when _links.next.href is present, the next request uses that href byte-for-byte against the same host — never rebuild it or append additional query parameters; stop when next is absent

delete_snapshot.job_rule: poll GET on exactly the returned href; terminal states are success and failure; a failed job carries error {code, message}; per-snapshot job failures are recorded and the remaining selected snapshots are still processed; an HTTP-level error on the DELETE itself aborts the run immediately

retention_selection.rule: a snapshot may be deleted only when it has an expiry_time that is at or before the as-of instant, has no owners (property absent or empty), and — when a snapmirror_label filter is given — its snapmirror_label equals the filter exactly (case-sensitive); results are ordered by create_time then name
retention_selection.note: snapshots may lack expiry_time or snapmirror_label entirely; missing properties must not crash strict-mode PowerShell and always exclude the snapshot from label-filtered selection

errors.client_rule: non-2xx responses raise an error whose message includes the ONTAP code and message and never the password

documented_delete_failures.1638543: Failed to delete snapshot because it has an owner
documented_delete_failures.1638555: The specified snapshot has not expired or is locked

error.message: The specified snapshot has not expired or is locked
